# Capo — Full Documentation for LLMs

> Capo is a song library for musicians. Organize your songs with chords and lyrics, build setlists as playlists, collaborate with your band through teams, and share setlists publicly via a link.

---

## Project Overview

Capo provides musicians of all levels with an organized song library featuring easy access to chords and lyrics for practice and performance. Songs are stored in the [ChordPro](https://www.chordpro.org/) open format, which embeds chord names directly into lyrics text.

The name "Capo" refers to the device that clamps onto a guitar neck to change the key while allowing open-chord shapes — a fitting metaphor for a tool that makes music more accessible.

---

## Feature Reference

### Song Library (`features/songs`)

The core feature. Users can:
- Browse their full song collection with search
- Filter by musical key (C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, B), BPM range, or active/archived status
- Group songs by key or artist
- View recently added songs on the dashboard

**Key types:**
```typescript
interface Song {
  id: string
  title: string
  artist: string
  key: string
  bpm: number
  lyrics?: string       // ChordPro-formatted content
  notes?: string
  tags?: string[]
  fontSize?: number
  transpose?: number
  capo?: number
  isDraft?: boolean
}

interface UserSongSettings {
  songId: string
  capo: number          // Per-user capo override
  transpose: number     // Per-user transpose override
  fontSize?: number
}

interface UserPreferences {
  minimalistLyricsView: boolean
  lyricsColumns: 1 | 2
}
```

**Public API hooks:** `useSongs`, `useUpdateSong`, `useUserSongSettings`, `useUpsertUserSongSettings`, `useEffectiveSongSettings`, `useAllUserSongSettings`, `useUserPreferences`, `useUpsertUserPreferences`

---

### Lyrics & Chords Editor (`features/lyrics-editor`)

A split-pane view for reading and editing songs:
- **Left pane**: CodeMirror editor with ChordPro syntax highlighting (Catppuccin theme)
- **Right pane**: Live-rendered chord sheet with styled chord names above lyrics
- Per-section chord color customization
- Multi-column layout (1 or 2 columns) stored as a user preference
- Minimalist view mode (hides UI chrome for performance/rehearsal)
- Transpose and capo controls per user per song

**Public API:** `LyricsView`, `RenderedSong`, `LazySongEditor`, `useLyricsSettings`

---

### Song Creation & Editing (`features/song-draft`)

Form-based UI for creating and editing songs:
- All metadata fields: title, artist, key, BPM, capo, transpose, notes, tags
- ChordPro lyrics editor with live preview
- Draft mode (songs not yet published to the library)

**Key type:**
```typescript
interface DraftSong {
  title: string
  artist: string
  key: string
  bpm: number
  capo?: number
  transpose?: number
  lyrics?: string
  notes?: string
  tags?: string[]
}
```

---

### Playlists (`features/playlists`)

Create and manage playlists of songs:
- Create, rename, and delete playlists
- Add/remove songs from a playlist
- Reorder songs within a playlist via drag-and-drop
- Each playlist can be set to public visibility with a unique share code

**Key types:**
```typescript
interface Playlist {
  id: string
  name: string
  description?: string
  date?: string
  songs: string[]           // Array of song IDs
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
  isDraft?: boolean
  visibility?: "private" | "public"
  allowGuestEditing?: boolean
  shareCode?: string
}

interface PlaylistWithSongs extends Omit<Playlist, "songs"> {
  songs: Song[]             // Fully resolved Song objects
}
```

**Public API hooks:** `usePlaylists`, `useCreatePlaylist`, `useUpdatePlaylist`, `useDeletePlaylist`, `useAddSongsToPlaylist`, `useReorderPlaylistSongs`

---

### Playlist Draft / Quick Add (`features/playlist-draft`)

A cart-style interface for assembling playlists on the fly:
- Add songs from the library to a floating draft tray
- Re-order draft songs before saving
- Save the draft as a brand-new playlist or append to an existing one
- Draft state is stored in React Context (scoped to the session)

**Public API:** `NewSongsProvider`, `useNewSongs` context hook

---

### Playlist Sharing (`features/playlist-sharing`)

Allows sharing a playlist publicly without requiring sign-in:
- Each public playlist has a unique `shareCode` (short alphanumeric string)
- Shareable link format: `/shared/[shareCode]`
- The shared view renders the full playlist with all song sheets
- No authentication required to view a shared playlist

---

### Teams & Collaboration (`features/teams`)

Multi-user team support:
- Create a named team; you become the owner
- Invite other users by email; they receive an invitation link
- Team members share a common song library and playlists
- Switch between personal context and a team context via the app header
- Manage pending invitations at `/dashboard/invitations`
- Team detail page shows members and open invitations

**Database schema fields on `teams` table:** `id`, `name`, `avatar_url`, `icon`, `created_by`, `is_public`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

---

### Dashboard (`features/dashboard`)

The main landing page after sign-in:
- Shows aggregate stats: total songs, total playlists, total teams
- Shows recently added songs for quick access
- Data is fetched server-side and passed as initial props to React Query

---

### Activity Tracking (`features/activity`)

Records user actions across the app (song views, playlist changes, etc.) to power the "recently viewed" experience and future personalization.

---

### Settings (`features/settings`)

User-configurable preferences:
- **Theme**: Light, dark, or system (via `next-themes`)
- **Language**: Switch UI locale; preference stored in a cookie (`NEXT_LOCALE`)
- Available locales are validated at build time with `pnpm i18n:validate`

---

### Authentication (`features/auth`)

- Google OAuth via Supabase Auth
- Server-side session management using Next.js server components
- `AuthStateProvider` listens for auth state changes and keeps React Query in sync
- Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to `/`

**Public API hooks:** `useSession`, `useSignInWithGoogle`, `useSignOut`, `useUser`

---

### App Context (`features/app-context`)

Global state shared across the entire app:
- Tracks the currently selected team (or personal context)
- Provides `AppContextProvider` and `useAppContext` hook
- Initial state is fetched server-side in the root layout and passed as props to avoid SSR mismatches

---

## Architecture: Feature-Based Architecture (FBA)

All business logic lives in `features/[feature-name]/`. Three rules:

1. **Organize by Feature** — code belongs in `features/[feature-name]/`
2. **Use Public APIs** — always import from `@/features/[name]` (never from internal paths)
3. **Export from index.ts** — each feature's contract is its `features/[name]/index.ts`

```
features/[name]/
├── components/      # UI components
├── hooks/           # Custom React hooks
├── types/           # TypeScript interfaces
├── utils/           # Utility functions
├── contexts/        # React Context state (if needed)
├── api/             # Server actions (actions.ts)
├── data/            # Data fetching utilities
├── __tests__/       # Tests and fixtures
└── index.ts         # PUBLIC API — the only import point
```

**Cross-feature import rule:**
```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT
import { usePlaylistDraft } from "@/features/playlist-draft"

// ❌ WRONG — never import from internal paths
import { usePlaylistDraft } from "@/features/playlist-draft/contexts/playlist-draft.context"
```

---

## Data Layer

- `lib/supabase/client.ts` — browser-side Supabase client
- `lib/supabase/server.ts` — server-side Supabase client (Next.js server components / actions)
- `lib/supabase/database.types.ts` — auto-generated types from schema (do not edit manually; regenerate with `pnpm types:generate`)
- `lib/supabase/apply-context-filter.ts` — shared query filter that scopes all queries to the current team or user
- Server actions live in `features/[name]/api/actions.ts`

**SSR hydration pattern** (avoids `HydrationBoundary` mismatches):
```tsx
// Server component (page)
const initialPlaylists = await api.getPlaylists(context).catch(() => [])
return <PlaylistsClient initialPlaylists={initialPlaylists} />

// Client component
export function PlaylistsClient({ initialPlaylists = [] }: { initialPlaylists?: Playlist[] }) {
  const { data: playlists = initialPlaylists } = usePlaylists()
  // ...
}
```

---

## App Routes

| Route | Access | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | Public | Sign-in page (Google OAuth) |
| `/shared/[shareCode]` | Public | Shared playlist view (no auth required) |
| `/teams/accept-invitation` | Public | Accept a team invitation |
| `/dashboard` | Protected | Main dashboard with stats |
| `/dashboard/songs` | Protected | Song library browser |
| `/dashboard/songs/[id]` | Protected | Song lyrics and chord editor |
| `/dashboard/playlists` | Protected | Playlist management |
| `/dashboard/teams` | Protected | Team list |
| `/dashboard/teams/new` | Protected | Create a new team |
| `/dashboard/teams/[id]` | Protected | Team detail (members, invitations) |
| `/dashboard/invitations` | Protected | Pending team invitations |

---

## SEO & Metadata

- All page titles use the template `"%s · Capo"` defined in the root layout
- Public pages (shared playlists) use `generateMetadata` to set dynamic OpenGraph and Twitter card tags
- All protected dashboard routes have `robots: { index: false, follow: false }` to prevent indexing

---

## Development Commands

```bash
pnpm dev              # Start dev server at http://localhost:3000
pnpm build            # Production build
pnpm typecheck        # TypeScript type checking
pnpm lint             # ESLint (zero warnings tolerance)
pnpm lint:fix         # ESLint auto-fix
pnpm test             # Run Jest tests
pnpm test:watch       # Jest watch mode
pnpm test:coverage    # Jest with coverage report
pnpm types:generate   # Regenerate lib/supabase/database.types.ts
pnpm i18n:validate    # Validate translation files
pnpm supabase start   # Start local Supabase (requires Docker)
pnpm supabase stop    # Stop local Supabase
```

---

## Code Style

Enforced by Prettier:
- No semicolons
- Double quotes
- Print width: 100 characters
- No trailing commas
- 2-space indentation

ESLint is strict: zero warnings allowed, `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` enforced.

---

## Testing

- Tests use Jest + `@testing-library/react` with jsdom
- Test files live in `features/[name]/__tests__/` and `lib/__tests__/`
- Fixtures (mock data) go in `__tests__/fixtures/`
- Coverage threshold: 80% branches/functions/lines/statements
- Run a single test file: `pnpm test -- --testPathPattern=features/songs/__tests__/hooks/use-songs.test.ts`

---

## Database

- Migrations in `supabase/migrations/`
- Always create migrations via CLI: `pnpm supabase migration new "describe_your_change"`
- Apply migrations: `pnpm supabase migration up --local`
- Regenerate types after schema changes: `pnpm types:generate`
- Local Supabase: API `localhost:54321`, DB `localhost:54322`, Studio `localhost:54323`
